text.title: Reasoning-First Architectures — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules: eval-before-spend, task-type budget, classifier-first router, human gate on irreversible, fall-through on budget cap.

rule.statement: Reasoning-model adoption MUST be preceded by an eval on ≥50 labelled examples that shows the reasoning route beats the standard route by a margin agreed in the acceptance contract. Shipping reasoning routing without this eval is forbidden.

rationale.source: OpenAI o-series, Anthropic Extended Thinking benchmarks 2026

rule.rationale: Cost is 10-50× per call. Without a measured lift on the actual task, the spend has no defence. Half of "reasoning needed" intuitions disappear under controlled comparison.

rule.statement: Thinking budget MUST be set per task-type bucket: 1K-4K for formatting / extraction, 4K-8K for code review / logic, 8K-32K for architecture planning, 32K-128K for theorem / deep research. A single global budget is forbidden.

rationale.source: Anthropic ET docs; OpenAI reasoning effort guide

rule.rationale: Thinking budget is an upper bound; the model decides how much to use. Too low truncates reasoning on hard tasks; too high wastes cost on simple ones. Per-bucket calibration captures actual variance.

rule.statement: A cheap classifier (haiku / nano / heuristic) MUST run first to decide whether the reasoning route is warranted. Direct-to-reasoning for every request is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Real-world workloads have a long tail of simple requests. Classifier overhead (~50ms, <$0.001) is negligible against the reasoning-model cost it saves.

rule.statement: When the reasoning-model output triggers an irreversible downstream action (deploy, payment, deletion), a human-review gate MUST sit between the model and the action. Reasoning confidence below the eval-set p10 score MUST escalate to human.
rule.rationale: Reasoning models hallucinate confident wrong answers on out-of-distribution inputs. Irreversible side effects amplify those failures. Gating limits blast radius.

rule.statement: The routing layer MUST have an explicit fall-through to the standard model when reasoning budget is exhausted (monthly spend cap hit) or the reasoning provider is unhealthy. Silent 5xx propagation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hard-coding the expensive route makes the product brittle to provider outages and cost overruns. Fall-through plus a degradation banner keeps the product alive at known quality cost.

example.title: Task-type budget selector
example.note: r2-task-type-budget — explicit per-bucket budgets, never a single global value.

example.title: Blanket reasoning
example.code: # r1 + r3 violations: ships every request to o3 without classifier, without eval evidence response = openai.chat.completions.create(model="o3", messages=[...], reasoning_effort="high")
example.note: No classifier, no eval gate, no budget per task. Burns money on requests that don't benefit.
